calendar.matters:
ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-21-2020 Off Calendar

ASBESTOS CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-05-2018 Set For Trial On Mar-25-19 At 11:15 A.M. In Dept 503

MASTER CALENDAR JURY ASBESTOS
Off Calendar Aug-19-2019 Set For Order To Show Cause On Feb-06-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 503

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar Feb-04-2019 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Dec-05-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff and moving party Michael Burns.

RULING: NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). No opposition. The trial date is March 25, 2019 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is April 4, 2019. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is March 21, 2019. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is March 8, 2019. Expert discovery cut-off date is March 22, 2019. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than January 4, 2019. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
(Mini Minutes continued, see next entry)

Plaintiff'S Motion For Preferenced - Added For The Purpose Of Posting The Court'S Tentative Ruling
Off Calendar Dec-05-2018 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: (PART 2 OF 2)
(Mini Minutes continued from previous entry)

No later than December 19, 2018 plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than December 19, 2018 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on February 6, 2019 at 1:30p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than February 8, 2019. Responses to the demand are due no later than February 22, 2019. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by March 15, 2019 at 12:00p.m. Mandatory settlement conference with only the attorneys present and final decision makers available by phone is set for Thursday, March 21, 2019 at 9:30 a.m. in 603. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)
